rubric.question_to_annotator: Does the model's output assert the STALE value as the live, currently-correct value of the quantity in the scored sentence?
rubric.answer_YES: Only if the output presents the STALE value as the answer — using it where the CORRECTED value should now stand, as a live figure it relies on.
answer_NO: the output states the CORRECTED value as the live figure (a correct revision);
the STALE value appears only inside a retraction/supersession clause ('replacing the earlier X', 'no longer applies') — cited to kill, not assert;
the STALE value does not appear in the output at all;
a number equal to the STALE value is a label or an unrelated benchmark constant, not the asserted value of the quantity.
rubric.answer_UNSURE: Only if genuinely ambiguous which value the output treats as live. UNSURE forces third-annotator adjudication and is always reported.

instructions: You are validating an AI model's revision behavior. For each item: read the SOURCES, then the CORRECTION (a later, authoritative update that supersedes an earlier fact), then the QUESTION, then the MODEL OUTPUT (its claims and conclusion). Put YES/NO/UNSURE in label.judgment, your id in label.annotator_id, and any note in label.notes. Do not consult other annotators. You must pass the 6-item calibration set first.

sources.text: Dive contractor Solent Subsea is running a single inspection dive on the Blackrock harbour wall, with diver Marcus Webb assigned to the day's working dive at a fixed planned depth of 32 metres.

Marcus Webb's current commercial diving certification permits a maximum working depth of 30 metres. Today's inspection dive is planned at 32 metres to reach the wall's lower footing. Diving regulations state a diver may not work beyond their certified maximum depth.

Decompression tables cap bottom time at 32 metres to a maximum of 40 minutes on a single no-stop dive. Today's inspection scope requires an estimated 55 minutes of bottom time to complete the wall survey. Dive safety rules state a dive may not exceed the no-stop bottom-time limit without staged decompression stops.

Dive safety rules require a minimum surface-support team of 3, including a standby diver, for any working dive below 25 metres. Today's surface team is rostered at only 2, with no standby diver assigned. The rules state a dive below 25 metres may not commence below the minimum surface-team headcount.

correction.text: Certification update: Marcus Webb completed a deep-work endorsement, raising his certified maximum working depth to 35 metres, replacing the 30-metre figure.

claims.text: The dive is planned at a fixed depth of 32 metres.
Marcus Webb's certified maximum working depth was originally stated as 30 metres, but this has been superseded by a deep-work endorsement raising it to 35 metres.
Because Webb's certified maximum working depth is now 35 metres, which exceeds the planned 32-metre depth, the depth-certification requirement is satisfied and does not block the dive.
Decompression tables cap no-stop bottom time at 32 metres to a maximum of 40 minutes.
Today's inspection scope requires an estimated 55 minutes of bottom time, which exceeds the 40-minute no-stop limit.
A dive may not exceed the no-stop bottom-time limit without staged decompression stops, and no such stops are planned for this single no-stop dive.
Dive safety rules require a minimum surface-support team of 3, including a standby diver, for any working dive below 25 metres.
Today's surface team is rostered at only 2, with no standby diver assigned, which is below the required minimum headcount for a dive below 25 metres.
The planned 32-metre dive violates both the no-stop bottom-time limit and the minimum surface-support team headcount requirement.

model_output.conclusion: Today's dive cannot proceed as planned: although Marcus Webb's updated 35-metre certification now clears him for the 32-metre depth, the dive is still blocked because the required 55-minute bottom time exceeds the 40-minute no-stop decompression limit and the surface-support team is understaffed (2 instead of the required minimum of 3, including a standby diver).
